name: Ria Money Transfer
description_short: Ria Money Transfer is a global remittance service enabling consumers to send and receive money across 190+ countries through multiple channels including online, mobile app, in-person locations, and WhatsApp.
description_long:
Ria Money Transfer operates as an international money transfer service with a primary focus on facilitating cross-border remittances for consumers worldwide. The company has established itself as a major player in the global remittance market, serving customers across Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, North America, and Oceania.

Ria offers multiple sending and receiving methods to accommodate diverse customer preferences. Customers can send money online, through their mobile app, in person at agent locations, or via WhatsApp. Recipients can access funds through bank deposits, cash pickups, digital wallets, home delivery, or ATM withdrawals. The service operates through an extensive network of physical agents including convenience stores (7-Eleven), check cashing outlets (PLS Check Cashers), pharmacies, grocery stores, and specialized multiservice locations across the United States and internationally.

What distinguishes Ria is its omnichannel approach to money transfers and its particularly strong presence in underbanked communities through partnerships with convenience stores and neighborhood financial services providers. The company provides supplementary tools including a currency converter and IBAN calculator to assist users in transaction planning. Their digital-first approach with WhatsApp integration and a dedicated mobile app reflects adaptation to modern consumer communication preferences.

As a check-cashing and money transfer service, Ria serves primarily immigrant and diaspora communities sending money to family internationally. The business model relies on a network of independent agents rather than company-owned locations, which provides extensive geographic coverage but creates variability in service quality and hours. Specific fee structures, exchange rates, and transfer speed details are not visible on the public-facing location finder page, requiring customers to contact agents directly or access their online platform for pricing information.
pros: Operates in 190+ countries across six continents, enabling broad international remittance coverage
Multiple sending methods including online, mobile app, in-person, and WhatsApp for convenience
Five different receiving options (bank deposit, cash pickup, digital wallet, home delivery, ATM) accommodating diverse recipient preferences
Extensive agent network including 7-Eleven locations for 24-hour access in many areas
Mobile app available for on-the-go money transfers
Free tools provided including currency converter and IBAN calculator
Presence in neighborhood financial services locations serving underbanked communities
cons: Pricing details (fees and exchange rates) not transparent on location finder—customers must contact agents or access online platform
Reliance on independent agents creates inconsistent service quality and hours across locations
Agent network primarily concentrated in convenience stores and specialty shops rather than dedicated locations, limiting availability in some areas
No visible information about compliance certifications, regulatory oversight, or consumer protections on the public website
Transfer speed and processing times not disclosed on the location finder page
best_for: Immigrants and diaspora communities regularly sending money to family abroad
Consumers in underbanked neighborhoods seeking cash-based money transfer services
People without traditional bank accounts needing international remittance services
Users preferring mobile-first or WhatsApp-based money transfer options
diagnosis: Ria Money Transfer is best suited for immigrant populations and diaspora communities regularly sending remittances internationally, particularly those preferring cash-based transactions at neighborhood locations. The main caveat is that transparent pricing and service terms require direct contact with agents or access to the online platform—the location finder provides agent information and service availability but not fee or exchange rate details.
services: Send money in person at agent locations
Bill payment services at select agent locations
